description_long: This object is a vintage phonograph, featuring a large, flared horn made of metal with a beige rim and a long, black conical body. The horn is attached to a rectangular wooden base with a dark, polished finish, which houses the mechanical components. On top of the base, a cylindrical drum and a tonearm mechanism are visible, suggesting the device plays sound from wax cylinders. Brass or gold-colored fasteners accent the corners of the wooden box, and a hand crank protrudes from one side, used to wind the internal mechanism. The overall design is ornate and classic, reminiscent of early 20th-century audio equipment. The object exudes a sense of historical craftsmanship and mechanical ingenuity.
description: A vintage phonograph with a large black and beige horn, mounted on a dark wooden box with brass accents and a hand crank.
description_view_0: The phonograph's horn dominates the view, with the beige rim clearly visible and the hand crank partially obscured.
description_view_1: The side profile highlights the length of the horn and the hand crank, with the cylinder and tonearm mechanism visible on top.
description_view_2: The rear angle shows the full extension of the horn and the back of the wooden base, with the hand crank more prominent.
description_view_3: The opposite side reveals the horn's attachment point and the top mechanism, with the hand crank on the left side.
